_title: The Body Hasn't Heard You Yet
_raw_content: You can understand your wound perfectly and still flinch every time someone gets close. That's because insight lives in the mind, but the pattern lives in the tissue. The nervous system doesn't update through comprehension — it updates through corrective experience. You have to feel safe in the place where you once felt destroyed. Not think safe. Feel it. That's the difference between knowing your story and being free of it.
slide_1_hook: You understand it. Your body doesn't.

slide_2.text: You've done the therapy. Named the pattern. Traced it back to its origin. And still — your chest tightens in the same rooms. Your throat closes around the same words. Understanding didn't finish the job.

slide_3.text: Insight is not integration. The mind can rewrite the narrative in an afternoon. The nervous system is still running code from decades ago. It doesn't speak in language. It speaks in tension. In breath. In the impulse to disappear before anyone sees you.

slide_4.text: Your body doesn't know the danger is over. No one told it. You told your journal. You told your therapist. You told yourself at 2am. But the tissue that braced — the fascia that hardened — the vagus nerve that learned to collapse — those are still waiting for the all-clear signal that never came.

slide_5.text: The nervous system doesn't update through comprehension. It updates through corrective experience. You have to stay present in the activation instead of exiting. You have to feel safe in the exact place where you once felt destroyed. Not think it. Feel it. In the body. At the speed the body can receive.

slide_6.text: Freedom is not knowing your story. Freedom is when your body stops telling it. When the pattern fires and your system doesn't follow. When presence replaces protection. That's not a mindset change. That's a nervous system that finally got the message — not through words, but through felt safety repeated until it became the new default.

slide_7_cta: Save this if your body needed to hear it before your mind did.
caption:
You can map every wound with surgical precision and still be completely run by it.

Because the body doesn't heal through understanding.
It heals through experience.

Through staying when it expects you to leave.
Through softening when it's braced for impact.
Through presence in the places that only ever knew abandonment.

The mind moves fast. The nervous system moves at the speed of trust.

Honor the lag. That's where the real work lives.

Save this for the moment you're wondering why you still react the way you do — even though you already know better.
